design_philosophy.core_loop: High-speed combat flight with instant directional control, weapon switching, and terrain navigation. Cycle of approach enemies → select appropriate weapon → evade with afterburners → destroy targets → reach extraction point.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Speed vs. control trade-off: afterburners enable evasion and high-speed flight but disable weapon fire. Tension between aggressive engagement and defensive maneuvering in constrained 3D spaces (tunnels, low-altitude flight).
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Piloting a powerful fighter craft (TV-202) in high-energy dogfights with instant responsiveness and no inertia simulation, delivering the sensation of fast, low-level flight without flight simulator complexity.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Explicitly simplified flight model (no inertia, instant turning) contrasts with contemporary flight simulators. Rejects simulation authenticity in favor of immediate, arcade-like responsiveness. Weapon-as-tool philosophy: 7 distinct weapons including rare secret option. Philosophy of accessibility: post-shareware version added mouse control due to user demand.

difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based checkpointing: 27 missions across 3 episodes, each mission can be retried independently. No explicit mention of mid-mission checkpoints.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: No documented difficulty settings. Weapon variety provides tactical choice (guns, blasters, rockets, homing missiles, secret weapon) but inherent challenge appears fixed. Terrain collision survivable to some degree, suggesting forgiving damage model.

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon switching system (7 weapons available throughout): guns, blasters, rockets, homing missiles, rare secret weapon, and afterburners as selectable tool. Afterburners function as speed-oriented utility rather than traditional upgrade.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Episode-gated progression: Episode 1 distributed as shareware (3 worlds); Episodes 2-3 locked behind commercial purchase. Hidden bonus mission in CD-ROM version unlocks after main plot completion.
replayability_hooks: Secret tunnels embedded in levels encourage exploration
No documented time limits allow for non-linear mission approach

world_building_techniques: Pre-rendered 3D cutscenes (70 MB added in CD-ROM version)
Mission briefing structure (27 discrete objectives across episodes)
Environmental narrative: hidden tunnels, secret ships suggest ongoing conflict and discovery
Plot revelation arc: Episode 3 reveals Xenocidic Initiative (X.I.) supercomputer as war architect; hidden mission reveals X.I. digitized insane pilot Sy Wickens

boss_design.philosophy: Milestone encounters marking episode/planetary progression. Each planet culminates in mandatory boss destruction before proceeding. Final mission targets the X.I. supercomputer—the narrative antagonist revealed through story progression.
notable_bosses: Planetary bosses (one per world, three worlds per episode, 9 total)
Xenocidic Initiative (X.I.) supercomputer - Proxima Seven (final boss, narrative focal point)
Unknown metamorphic force (hidden bonus mission, CD-ROM version)

faction_indicators: Alliance of Space-Faring Alien Races (ASFAR) - Earth's supposedly allied faction that betrays humanity
Player faction implied: human defenders piloting TV-202 fighters
danger_signals: Sudden ASFAR fleet attack on Earth initiates existential war
X.I. supercomputer orchestrates conflict from Proxima Seven
Metamorphosis of unknown planet (hidden mission) signals external existential threat
environmental_storytelling: Secret tunnels discoverable during missions hint at hidden base infrastructure
Hidden ships embedded in levels suggest wreckage or cached assets from prior conflict
Multi-planetary campaign structure (9 worlds across 3 episodes) implies sprawling war theater
Low-altitude flight through terrain reinforces military reconnaissance/strike mission tone
